subtitleStructure.text: Deciphering the mechanisms of galaxy formation and evolution through integrated dynamical modeling and multi-wavelength observations.

about.description:
Welcome to the homepage of the galaxy structure and dynamics group. We are an independent research group in Shanghai Astronomical Observatory led by Ling Zhu. Our research mostly focuses on galaxy structure and dynamics, including but not limited to

Development of chemo-dynamics models
Galaxy structure formation and galaxy assembly history
Galaxy structure formation in different environments
Probing dark matter distribution in nearby galaxies combining stellar, GCs, PNe and HI kinematics
Supermassive black hole mass estimation
Recently, we developed a population-orbit superposition method, which can simultaneously fit the stellar luminosity distribution, kinematic, age and metallicity maps from IFU observations. Combining dynamics and stellar populations provides us unprecedented information of 'when' and 'how' the structures in a galaxy formed. We thus have been able to uncover the ancient merger events in nearby galaxies with IFU observations.

opportunities.content: We are constantly seeking motivated postdocs and students who are interested in galaxy dynamics and evolution. Our group provides a collaborative environment with access to state-of-the-art computational resources and world-class observational data.
